data.title: Justin Sun turns against WLFI: A post-sales dispute over a commercial lease
data.description:
On April 12th, Sun Yuchen published a strongly worded public statement on X, accusing WLFI of... The smart contract has a built-in blacklist function, which can unilaterally freeze any holder's assets without notice, explanation, or any appeal process.

WLFIThe official account responded quickly:" Does anyone still believe Justin Sun? Playing the victim to cover up his misconduct. See you in court, buddy. What is Justin Sun buying? To understand Justin Sun's current anger, we need to first understand what he was buying in the first place. In March 2023, the SEC formally filed a lawsuit against Justin Sun and three of his companies, alleging illegal sale of unregistered securities, manipulation of market trading volume, and hiring Lindsay Lohan. Celebrities like Lohan promoted the token but concealed the payment relationship; they were accused of illegally profiting $31 million, and also faced continued pressure from US regulators. Since 2023, Justin Sun has been avoiding entry into the United States due to legal risks. This was the situation he faced at the time. In November 2024, the winds shifted, and Trump won the election. Three weeks later, Justin Sun, through a wallet linked to Huobi, purchased $3,000,000 worth of WLFI tokens at a price of $0.015 per token, becoming the largest individual investor. At the time, WLFI sales were dismal, totaling only $21 million. Justin Sun stepped in to save the day, and the token price immediately surged by over 50%. The project team subsequently appointed him as an advisor.

2025year1Month19On the day before Trump's inauguration, Justin Sun announced an additional4500

2025year5Month, Justin Sun became the largest holder of Trump Coin" leaf="">"

2026year3Month,SECformally reached a settlement with Sun Yuchen, and its affiliated companies only need to pay1000

Who needs this deal more?

A core logic in "The Art of the Deal" is that the person who needs the deal less always holds the pricing power.

In this game, Justin Sun faces federal litigation, a Department of Justice investigation, and the risk of being ousted from the entire US capital market. If the case proceeds, the alleged amount of illegal activity is $31 million, with potential civil penalties starting at around $60 million. The criminal risk, however, means imprisonment, a problem that money cannot solve. Moreover, every day that Tron's listing on Nasdaq is delayed means one less day of window for its listing.

2025Year9month, frozen by WLFIfrozen for more than1

37After-sales liquidation of Tianhou

2026Year3month

3737 days later, he posted that statement targeting Xon. The timing was not a coincidence. Just days before his statement, the market discovered that WLFI had staked 50 billion governance tokens on the Dolomite lending protocol, lending out approximately [amount missing]. leaf="">7500$10,000 stablecoin——AndDolomiteco-founder and also serves asWLFIadvisor andCTO。

The project team used its own issued tokens as collateral to borrow money, transferring funds between different parties,WLFISubsequently, it plummeted to an all-time low. This is not only for the residual value that has shrunk to $43 million, but also a precise deterrent. He chose to launch his attack at the crucial moment when problems arose, making his own victim narrative more believable. Settling the score isn't complicated. He probably only lost about $20 million buying Trump Memecoin. The commercial aspect was cut short, while the compliance aspect was fully realized. He didn't lose to Trump, but to WLFI's operations team. He found a bigger protector, but those under that protector conveniently skimmed off his money. The Quietest Loser

WLFIThe ability to sell at such a high price relies on the power premium created by the endorsement of the Trump family; the ability to maintain this price for so long relies on the market signal of continuous endorsement from Justin Sun, the largest individual investor.

As the largest individual investor and advisor of the project, Justin Sun, when investing tens of millions of dollars, theoretically had far more knowledge of the project's code architecture and governance logic than the average person.

The Quietest Loser

As the largest individual investor and advisor of the project, Justin Sun, when investing tens of millions of dollars, theoretically had far more knowledge of the project's code architecture and governance logic than the average person.

At the time, this centralized control was seen as a necessary tool for maintaining project stability; today, however, when these tools are used to lock up his own assets, it officially becomes a pretext for attack. This is no longer a debate about the spirit of decentralization, but a forced game about asset liquidity. In this game, credit itself becomes a priced resource, and tokens are more like a carrier circulating around it. When disagreements arise in allocation, the conflict becomes explicit. Now, that $43 million remains frozen, as the price of the coin shrinks daily. In the crypto world, the phrase "See you in court" usually stops at social media, and the two sides will most likely end up making a behind-the-scenes compromise. Those ordinary investors who entered the market at the high of the US dollar (0.33) never had their profits or losses recorded in the overall account of this core game. The losses they suffered were neither entry fees nor bargaining chips, but merely fuel in the process of monetizing power.

data.title: Ultraman's mansion attacked twice within 48 hours; anti-AI anger spreads globally.
data.description:
"Why did we knowingly create something like this?" The question was posed by a 20-year-old American named Moreno-Gama. A relatively unknown figure online, he had been quietly lurking in a group called PauseAI (an anti-AI organization) for two years, posting a total of 34 messages. Last December, he left his final message: "The time is almost up. It's time to take action." The moderator warned him against inciting violence, and he never spoke again.

A screenshot from the security camera footage outside Altman's house, with Moreno-Gama's face blurred

Who could have imagined that four months later, at 3:45 AM local time on April 10th, he would carry a homemade Molotov cocktail and smash it in front of the $65 million mansion of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman.

Fortunately, the Molotov cocktail bounced off the iron gate, only charring a corner of the door; no one was injured. Ultraman and his family were still sleeping inside at the time. An hour later, police arrested the young man outside OpenAI headquarters, who was attempting the same trick again, carrying another unexploded Molotov cocktail. The fact that the Molotov cocktail bounced off was indeed fortunate, but it was by no means a false alarm. While it didn't burn down the iron gate, it clearly told everyone that some people's fear and anger towards AI had evolved from online complaints to the point of actually picking up Molotov cocktails in real life. And soon reality proved that Moreno-Gama wasn't the last.

48 hours, two attacks, a bullseye forming

After the incident, the anti-AI organization PauseAI issued a statement almost immediately: "We wish Sam Altman, his family, and all those affected safe. PauseAI exists because we believe everyone has the right to safety, including Sam Altman and his loved ones. Violence against anyone goes against everything we stand for."

See that larger piece of soil

In March 2023, the Future of Life Institute (FLI) released a famous open letter entitled "Pause Large-Scale AI Experiments." The letter warned that "AI labs have fallen into an out-of-control race." They called on the entire industry to pause AI development for six months.

See that larger piece of soil

In March 2023, the Future of Life Institute (FLI) released a famous open letter entitled "Pause Large-Scale AI Experiments." The letter warned that "AI labs have fallen into an out-of-control race." They called on the entire industry to pause AI development for six months.

Every technological revolution in history has seen similar violent incidents at some point, which were then attributed by the mainstream narrative to the fanaticism and madness of a minority, and gradually submerged in the dust of time.

In the mainstream narrative of technology, progress is inevitable, growing pains are temporary, and the future will be better. This rhetoric is respectable enough, but also vague enough. Who exactly has taken the benefits of AI technology development?

Who bears the brunt of those real costs? Moreno-Gama is an extreme example; his actions offer no justification. However, condemning violence and acknowledging the underlying causes of violence are not contradictory. The purpose of technology is to improve the lives of the majority. If we cannot draw clear ethical and safety boundaries for AI, nor find a fair way to share the benefits of technology, then all the current controversies and resistance surrounding AI are likely just the beginning.

data.title: Behind the Circle freeze controversy: Where are the power boundaries of dollar stablecoins?
data.description:
If one day you find that your stablecoins suddenly cannot be transferred out, withdrawn, or even explained—At that moment, you will realize: the money you thought belonged to you may not actually belong to you.

This is not hypothetical.

Freezing, More Than Just Freezing

Many people underestimate the impact of "freezing," thinking it only means a certain address is immobilized. But this incident has proven that what is frozen is never just an address, but an entire flow of funds.

Many people underestimate the impact of "freezing," thinking it only means a certain address is inactive. But this incident has proven that what is frozen is never just a single address, but an entire flow of funds.

Can be manipulated

So the question is:Are you using "on-chain dollars" or "freezeable dollars"?Can be manipulated

So the question is:Are you using "on-chain dollars" or "freezeable dollars"?

A "gray area" that is being opened

After this incident, the focus of industry discussion has become:

  • Who has the right to freeze?

    • Who has the right to freeze?

      • Is the basis for the freeze publicly available? Is on-chain transparent review required? How is erroneous freezes compensated? In other words, the issue of stablecoins is shifting from a "technical issue" to a "power issue." Many might think this is simply a power struggle between institutions. However, in reality, if you hold stablecoins, trade with stablecoins, and participate in on-chain activities, you are already part of this system. This incident leaves behind a very direct question: What can you do if your money is mistakenly frozen one day? This discussion surrounding the "power boundaries" of dollar-denominated stablecoins is far from over. Stablecoins are becoming a fundamental vehicle for global capital flows. And any vehicle, once it possesses the power to "freeze," ceases to be merely a tool. It itself becomes a power that needs to be constrained.

data.title: Fake Cross-Chain Proof Exploit Hits Polkadot Bridged DOT on Ethereum as Attacker Mints 1 Billion Tokens and Dumps Market
data.description:
Bridged Dot Drained After Fake Proof Bypasses Cross-Chain Security Checks

A vulnerability in a cross-chain bridge tied to Polkadot has led to the creation of 1 billion unauthorised DOT tokens on Ethereum, triggering a sharp price drop and forcing exchanges to halt trading activity.

The exploit targeted the Hyperbridge gateway system, where an attacker successfully submitted a falsified cryptographic proof that was incorrectly accepted as valid.

Once the forged message passed verification, it triggered contract logic that handed administrative control of the bridged token contract to the attacker’s wallet.

With that access, they minted 1 billion DOT tokens, far exceeding the circulating bridged supply at the time, and moved quickly to liquidate them.

How A Fake Proof Took Control Of The Token Contract

The incident stemmed from a failure in how the Hyperbridge system validated cross-chain messages.

The attacker’s fake proof was treated as legitimate, allowing automated contract execution without human intervention.

The compromised function changed the admin rights of the bridged DOT contract on Ethereum, effectively giving the attacker full control over minting permissions.

According to blockchain security firm CertiK, this allowed the attacker to seize control before issuing the inflated token supply.

#CertiKInsight 🚨

We have seen an exploit on the @hyperbridge gateway contract. https://t.co/h27iDm1JGd

The attacker slipped through a forged message to change the admin of Polkadot token contract on Ethereum and profited ~$237K from minting and selling 1B tokens.

Stay… pic.twitter.com/3t2n4uq5hy

— CertiK Alert (@CertiKAlert) April 13, 2026

Onchain analysts from Lookonchain noted that the attacker then deployed the newly minted tokens into decentralised markets in a single sweep, draining liquidity almost immediately.

Polkadot(@Polkadot) has been exploited. 🚨

The attacker minted 1B $DOT and dumped it all in a single transaction for 108.2 $ETH($237K).https://t.co/4pStYrGb8ypic.twitter.com/wRplAWNnBg

— Lookonchain (@lookonchain) April 13, 2026

1 Billion Tokens Minted And Dumped In Single Move

After gaining control, the attacker minted 1 billion bridged DOT tokens, roughly 2,805 times higher than the existing supply at that moment.

The tokens were then dumped into Uniswap, where liquidity pools were rapidly drained.

The sale generated about 108.2 ETH, valued at approximately $237,000, before funds were routed through Odos Router V3 and returned to the attacker’s wallet.

Someone just minted 1,000,000,000 bridged $DOT out of thin air.

That's equal to 48% of the native Polkadot token total supply, conjured from a vulnerability, then dumped for 108 $ETH (~$237K) before anyone could blink.

Here's what happened

- The attacker funded their EOA… pic.twitter.com/EK80KhqBNG

— capie.eth (@scoutingcapie) April 13, 2026

As the artificial supply flooded the market, the price of bridged DOT collapsed from around $1.22 to near $1, with some trackers showing it falling to tiny fractions of a cent during peak impact.

Exchanges React As Trading Controls Are Triggered

The disruption prompted immediate precautionary measures across trading platforms.

Upbit suspended DOT deposits and withdrawals while the situation was assessed, citing concerns over ongoing volatility and token integrity.

폴카닷(DOT) 거래 유의 종목 지정 안내

폴카닷(DOT/KRW, DOT/BTC)이 DAXA 회원사들에 의해 거래 유의 종목으로 지정되었습니다.

Polkadot(DOT/KRW, DOT/BTC) has been flagged with investment warning by Digital Asset eXchange Association(DAXA).

🔗 Discover more: https://t.co/Aa6bK49wdh

— Upbit Korea (@Official_Upbit) April 13, 2026

Other platforms are understood to be monitoring exposure to bridged DOT tokens on Ethereum as investigations continue, with temporary restrictions expected to remain in place until further clarity is reached.

Why The Hyperbridge Model Failed To Prevent The Attack

Hyperbridge, built as a trust-minimised interoperability system, relies heavily on cryptographic proofs rather than manual validation.

In this case, that design became the entry point for the exploit.

Developers had structured the system so that verified messages automatically trigger contract execution.

However, the attacker managed to generate a forged proof that the system accepted without detecting manipulation, enabling unauthorised administrative changes.

Hyperbridge has since paused its Ethereum gateway contract while the vulnerability is reviewed.

Polkadot Ecosystem Remains Unaffected Despite Bridged Token Crash

The core Polkadot network, including its parachains and native DOT, has not been impacted.

Officials clarified that only DOT tokens bridged to Ethereum via Hyperbridge were affected, while other bridging routes remain intact.

We’re aware of an issue affecting @hyperbridge's Ethereum gateway contract.

The exploit only affects DOT on Ethereum that is bridged through Hyperbridge and does not affect DOT in the Polkadot ecosystem, or DOT bridged through other bridges.

Polkadot, its parachains, and…

— Polkadot (@Polkadot) April 13, 2026

The situation highlights a contained but significant breakdown within a specific interoperability layer rather than the broader Polkadot ecosystem itself, as teams continue investigating the breach and assessing potential fixes.

data.title: WLFI Brings Justin Sun To Court After Sun Accuses The Platform Of Using The Crypto Community As Its Personal ATM Machine
data.description:
WLFI Uses Customers As Personal ATMs?

Trump's World Liberty Financial (WLFI) is threatening to bring Justin Sun to court after the crypto billionaire publicly accused the platform of embedding hidden controls in its token contract.

The dispute escalated rapidly after WLFI dismissed Sun’s claims as baseless and warned it was prepared to take the matter to court.

In the statement, WLFI said

“Justin’s favorite move is playing the victim while making baseless allegations to cover up his own misconduct. We have the contracts. We have the evidence. We have the truth. See you in court.”

Does anyone still believe @justinsuntron ?

Justin’s favorite move is playing the victim while making baseless allegations to cover up his own misconduct.

Same playbook, different target. WLFI isn't the first.

We have the contracts. We have the evidence. We have the truth.

See…

— WLFI (@worldlibertyfi) April 12, 2026

The response came within hours of Sun’s public accusations, marking a sharp escalation in what had been a simmering conflict between one of WLFI’s largest investors and the project’s operators.

Sun-who has invested more than $75 million in WLFI-claims the project embedded a blacklisting function in its smart contract—effectively treating the crypto community as its own personal ATM.

I have always been an ardent supporter of President Trump and his crypto friendly policy.

As an early supporter who invested heavily in World Liberty Financial, I did so because I believed in the vision that was presented to the public: a decentralized finance platform that…

— H.E. Justin Sun 👨‍🚀 🌞 (@justinsuntron) April 12, 2026

He described the mechanism as a “trap door,” arguing that it contradicts WLFI’s positioning as a decentralized finance platform. According to Sun, governance processes used to justify such actions were neither transparent nor fair, with outcomes that appeared predetermined.

Dispute rooted in 2025 wallet blacklist

The conflict traces back to September 2025, when WLFI blacklisted a wallet linked to Sun containing more than 500 million WLFI tokens.

Lol 😂
WLFI took Justin Sun's money and locked his tokens forever 🤣

There's no scam like a Trump scam https://t.co/iSny9dHQu4

— 𝔽𝕋 (@FTPager) April 10, 2026

The ban comes after the project suspected Sun of suspicious activities involving transfers routed through Sun's crypto exchange. Back then, Sun has denied any wrongdoing and now has used this incident as evidence of WLFI's tyrannical acts.

Blockchain data cited in reports suggests the ongoing restriction of the wallet may have resulted in losses exceeding $80 million for Sun.

Sun's accusations come at a time when WLFI faces broader scrutiny over its use of WLFI as collateral on Dolomite, a decentralized lending protocol. Notably, the protocol is tied to one of the venture's advisers.

After the news broke regarding WLFI's sketchy doings, the price of the WLFI token dropped roughly 76% from its all time high, plummeting to its all new low of $0.08.

$WLFI continues to defend the $0.10 level as strong support. While the market hesitates, price holds steady. When the trend shifts, it will be among the leaders of the move. pic.twitter.com/zI6zXN8EDX

— Clazn (@clazn0x) February 26, 2026

Critics argue that such practices introduce liquidity risks and potential conflicts of interest, particularly given the project’s internal ties to the platform.

WLFI has defended its approach, stating its positions remain well above liquidation thresholds and describing itself as an “anchor borrower.” The team has also begun repaying part of its loan and is preparing governance proposals aimed at improving token distribution.

A defining clash over DeFi control

While Sun is prepared to go all out to back its claims, but he was prepared to separate the accusations from President Trump himself, opening his statement by reaffirming support for Trump and directing criticism at "the bad actors at WLFI."

Justin Sun vs WLFI

- crypto billionaire, founder of TRON, early believer in the Trump narrative

late 2024: invests $30M into World Liberty Financial

- scales position to ~$75M
- becomes one of the largest holders
- publicly supports the project

September 2025: sends ~$9M in… pic.twitter.com/30r8bt94Lz

— StarPlatinum (@StarPlatinum_) April 12, 2026

The dispute highlights a broader issue in DeFi: whether platforms that promise decentralization can justify retaining centralized controls over user assets.

With legal action now on the table, the outcome of this conflict could shape how investors assess trust, governance, and risk in high-profile crypto projects.

data.title: Ethereum enters reset period in Q1: A comprehensive overview of the ecosystem and key development areas.
data.description:
Written by: The DeFi Report Translated by: Glendon, Techub News

Since 2022, the Ethereum network has transitioned to a Proof-of-Stake (PoS) mechanism and actively advanced its Layer 2 scaling roadmap, becoming the foundation for nearly $200 billion in stablecoins and RWAs. In the process, while on-chain fee capture has significantly decreased, Ethereum has continued to strengthen L1 security and keep ETH's inflation rate below 1%.

Meanwhile, Ethereum has recently established an early lead in the race towards "quantum computing readiness." This article reviews the Ethereum ecosystem's performance in the first quarter and future focus areas. Let's begin.

Operational Performance

Real Economic Value

Data Source: DeFi Report

Real Economic Value (REV) is measured by the payment status of base fees, priority fees, MEV (Maximum Extractable Value), and Blob fees in the L1 block space. Base fees and Blob fees are burned by the network, thus accumulating value for passive ETH holders. Priority fees and MEV belong to validators and stakers.

Data Source: DeFi Report

Real Economic Value (REV) is measured by the payment status of base fees, priority fees, MEV (Maximum Extractable Value), and Blob fees in the L1 block space. Base fees and Blob fees are burned by the network, thus accumulating value for passive ETH holders. Priority fees and MEV belong to validators and stakers.

  • Flashbots: $43 million

  • Maple Finance: $25.3 million

    • Why is GDP important?

    Ethereum L1 GDP vs. ETH price comparison

    Ethereum L1 GDP and ETH price have a potential correlation. To some extent, network GDP can provide a basis for network valuation, so we continue to closely monitor this indicator. Real-World Assets (RWA) On-Chain Status

    On-chain Real-World Assets = Tokenized representations of physical and traditional financial assets issued, traded, and managed on Ethereum L1. Stablecoins are counted separately. Data from rwa.xyz. Currently, there are $6.5 billion in tokenized US Treasury bonds on Ethereum L1, a 37% increase in the first quarter and an 85% year-over-year increase.

    ... BlackRock's BUIDL fund grew 94% this quarter, but declined 44% year-over-year. The reason for the year-over-year decline is that some assets have been transferred to Avalanche, Solana, BNB chains, and multiple L2 networks. Across all chains, BUIDL's assets under management (AUM) increased by 11% year-over-year. Furthermore, commodities ($5.1 billion) accounted for 31% of the total on-chain RWA, growing 48% this quarter and 312% year-over-year. This category was primarily driven by tokenized gold (accounting for 99% of tokenized commodities). Tether and Paxos are the largest issuers of tokenized gold. Currently, publicly traded stocks account for only 2% of the total on-chain RWA (approximately $349 million), a 22% increase from the first quarter. Overall, the total RWA on Ethereum L1 currently exceeds $16 billion, representing a 27% quarterly increase and a 225% year-over-year increase. The cost of generating $1 of real economic value is calculated as: Average daily issuance paid to Ethereum validators / REV. This metric measures the efficiency with which the blockchain converts security expenditures into REV. When this value is greater than $1, it indicates that the network's security expenditures exceed its REV gains. Ethereum's cost to generate $1 of REV (supply-side issuance) in the first quarter was $13.79, the highest level since the metric was first tracked. This figure rose 20% this quarter, indicating that the network needs more token issuance (i.e., higher inflation) to keep it secure relative to the actual value generated this quarter—meaning that network costs/expenses are increasing relative to revenue.

    L2 Payments to L1

    L2 Payments to L1 = L1 Base Fees Paid by L2 to Ethereum L1 + Blob Submission Fees. The L2 platforms covered include: Arbitrum, Base, Blast, Bob, Linea, Optimism, Scroll, Soneium, Starknet, Worldchain, and Zksync.

    ... In the first quarter, L2 paid a total of only $135,000 to L1 (the lowest quarterly level on record), a decrease of 82% quarter-over-quarter and 89% year-over-year. What are the reasons behind this data? The Pectra upgrade, implemented in May 2025, expanded L2-level Blob throughput after the initial introduction of Blobs in the Dencun upgrade in March 2024. Subsequently, on January 7th of this year, the Blob target was further increased, expanding data availability capacity to 14 Blobs per block. Currently, the average actual usage per block is only 3-4 Blobs, therefore, L2 payments to L1 continue to be compressed, and this pressure will likely persist until L2 demand expands sufficiently to consistently exceed the Blob target. If sufficiently strong demand growth occurs in the future (possibly waiting for the next bull market), higher Blob fees and priority pricing mechanisms may emerge, thereby enhancing the value capture capability of the L1 layer. This is a typical example of supply and demand: the current growth rate of L2 Blob space supply far exceeds the growth rate of demand. Key Conclusions: Ethereum L1 has achieved self-disruption through its own L2 scaling roadmap. This is crucial for network expansion, improving user experience, and enhancing economic scalability. The cost is that the continued migration of user activity to L2 leads to reduced competition for L1 block space, resulting in a decrease in on-chain fee revenue for validators and stakers. The key question now is: as L2 flourishes, must Ethereum L1 simultaneously increase its fees? In other words, even if L1 fee capture fails to keep pace with L2 growth, can Ethereum still achieve greater success and drive up its overall valuation through the L2 roadmap (such as introducing traditional financial L2 with traditional assets)? This topic will be explored in more detail later in the report.

    ETH Staking Status

    As of March 31, 2026, a total of 38.7 million ETH were staked on the network, representing 31.7% of the circulating supply (a record high). What impact does this have on token holders?

    ... More ETH staked = If on-chain fees do not increase proportionally, then the more ETH staked, the lower the staking yield. This is because an increase in staking leads to a relatively smaller increase in new token issuance. If on-chain fee revenue and/or token burning cannot make up for this shortfall, the staking yield will decrease, while network inflation will increase—a trend we have observed in the first quarter and several previous quarters. StablecoinsStablecoin Supply

    Stablecoin Supply

    The total supply of stablecoins on Ethereum L1 reached $180 billion at the end of this quarter, a decrease of 0.55% quarter-over-quarter, but an increase of 30% year-over-year.

    Distribution of major stablecoins: USDT (Tether): 53% of supply, down 6% in Q1 but up 27% year-over-year; USDC (Circle/Coinbase): 30% of supply, up 5% in Q1 and 38% year-over-year; USDS (Sky): 4.6% of supply, up 50% in Q1 and 87% year-over-year; USDe (Ethena): 3.3% of supply, down 7% in Q1 but up 12% year-over-year; USDPY ​​(PayPal): Now accounts for over 1.5% of supply (approximately $2.9 billion), up 8% in Q1 and 360% year-over-year.

    Key Conclusions

    In the last bear market, the stablecoin supply peaked on April 1, 2022, nearly five months after Bitcoin's price peak (USDC didn't reach its peak until July 2022). Currently, the stablecoin supply has been consolidating for about five months, reflecting a stagnation in crypto market inflows and a sharp decline in reflective yields in DeFi. Therefore, we believe the stablecoin supply may have already reached its peak for this year.

    Stablecoin Circulation Velocity

    Effective stablecoin circulation velocity measures the average daily turnover rate of each US dollar of stablecoin supply on the blockchain. This metric eliminates interference factors such as wash trading and revolving transfers, truly reflecting economic activity. It is calculated as daily net US dollar transfers divided by circulating supply. An increase in this value indicates enhanced economic activity on Ethereum L1.

    The average stablecoin circulation velocity in the first quarter was 0.019, a decrease of 30% quarter-over-quarter and 10% year-over-year.

    This means that, on average, only about 2% of the stablecoin supply was "turning over" daily. In comparison, Solana turned over an average of 6.8% of its stablecoin supply daily in the first quarter. Key Conclusions: Despite the decrease in on-chain velocity of flow due to risk aversion, Ethereum remains the core hub for on-chain assets (the chain with the highest TVL), while Solana has become the preferred network for high-frequency trading and high-speed circulation. Stablecoin velocity of flow is a key indicator for tracking this trend.

    Token Economics

    Network Dilution Rate

    Net Dilution Rate = Protocol New Issuance - Burned ETH / Circulating Supply (Annualized). A negative net dilution rate benefits ETH holders (value accumulation). A positive net dilution rate dilutes the equity of ETH holders (unstakers).

    Network Dilution Rate = Protocol New Issuance - Burned ETH / Circulating Supply (Annualized).

    ... The net dilution rate (applicable to non-stakers holding ETH) for the first quarter was 0.84% ​​(annualized), a 4.4% increase this quarter and a 55% year-over-year increase. Despite lower on-chain fees and increased issuance, the network inflation rate (annualized) over the past year remains only 0.79% (lower than Bitcoin). Key Conclusions: We believe this is the most crucial chart for understanding Ethereum. The reason is that on-chain fees paid to validators and stakers continue to decline, and a reversal is unlikely in the short term. Meanwhile, ETH staking continues to climb. This means that Ethereum's security remains strong, but L1 layer suppliers (stakers) are receiving the lowest level of compensation since the merger. This is very noteworthy because Ethereum is proving it can: Scaling through L2 and amplifying network effects (e.g., Robinhood L2 has entered the testnet); Maintaining or even enhancing L1 security even with fees paid to validators/stakers at historic lows; and Maintaining a lower ETH inflation rate than Bitcoin. We believe this reinforces Ethereum's narrative as a "store of value" (low inflation) asset that also pays yields (2.8% in Q1) while securing hundreds of billions of dollars in value.

    DeFi

    DEX Trading Volume

    DEX trading volume on Ethereum L1 decreased by 43% in the first quarter, but increased by 24% year-over-year. Total DEX trading volume on L2 networks decreased by 29% quarter-over-quarter, but increased by 10% year-over-year. Among them, the Base chain's trading volume decreased by 19% quarter-over-quarter, accounting for 30% of all L2 trading volume this quarter (compared to 59% in the previous quarter). Overall, L2 networks accounted for 45% of all Ethereum DEX trading volume this quarter, up from 36% in the previous quarter. Notably, the "risk-averse" environment actually led to more trading activity flowing back to L1. In summary, Ethereum's L1 and L2 DEX trading volume reached $330 billion in the first quarter, surpassing Solana's $286 billion. Active Loans in DeFi

    Total active loans on Ethereum L1 decreased by 22% in the first quarter, but increased by 35% year-over-year.

    • Aave has $16.5 billion in active loans (down 24% quarter-over-quarter), accounting for 73% of the L1 market share.

      • Aave has $16.5 billion in active loans (down 24% quarter-over-quarter), accounting for 73% of the L1 market share.

        • Morpho has $3.8 billion in active loans (a 22% quarter-over-quarter increase) and a market share of 16.7%. On L2, total active lending decreased by 10% quarter-over-quarter but increased by 66% year-over-year. All L2 networks combined account for 14% of total active lending in the Ethereum ecosystem.

          L2 Ecosystem

          L2 Fees

          Total L2 network fees decreased by 46% in the first quarter and increased by 6% year-over-year. For the full year of 2025, L2 networks generated a total of $117 million in fees, with Base accounting for 66% of the market share. The total fees generated by L2 networks in this quarter were equivalent to 31% of Ethereum L1 fees, significantly lower than the 52% in the fourth quarter of 2025.

          L2 network fees decreased by 46% in the first quarter and increased by 6% year-over-year.

          ...

          L2 Active Users

          The average daily active addresses of the L2 network (total) decreased by 28% in the first quarter and by 18% year-over-year. This trend has been declining since last summer, but may have bottomed out this quarter.

          This quarter, the average daily active addresses of L2 were only 12% higher than those of L1, far below the 130% increase in the previous quarter. Among them, Base (which accounts for 56% of L2 active users) decreased by 31% quarter-over-quarter.

          L2 Transaction Volume

          The L2 network processed an average of 19.7 million transactions per day in the first quarter (8.9 times that of L1, and 14.5 times that of the previous quarter), a 13% decrease quarter-over-quarter, but still a 68% increase year-over-year. Base (down 22% quarter-over-quarter) accounted for 51% of all L2 transaction volume this quarter.

          L2 Stablecoins

          L2 Stablecoin Supply

          The total amount of stablecoins on the L2 network is currently $11 billion, a 1% decrease month-over-month and a 49% increase year-over-year. L2 stablecoins account for 6% of the total Ethereum L1 stablecoin supply.

          ... Base (3% quarterly growth) accounts for 42% of the L2 stablecoin supply; Arbitrum (10% quarterly decline) accounts for 36%. ETF and Asset Management Company Holdings ETF Net Flows ETFs saw a net outflow of $978 million in the first quarter, a decrease compared to the $1.4 billion net outflow in the fourth quarter of last year. Currently, all ETFs collectively hold 4.7% of the total Ethereum supply (up from 4.9% last quarter). Ethereum ETF assets under management (in ETH terms) have decreased by 17% from their peak on October 8th of last year. In comparison, Bitcoin ETFs are currently only down 5.5% from their peak. In the first quarter of 2025, DAT's ETH holdings reached a new all-time high, growing by 7% during the quarter. Currently, DAOs hold 5.9% of the total ETH supply, up from 5.5% in the previous quarter. Among them, Bitmine (BMNR), a leading company in the DAT sector, currently holds over 4.6 million ETH, a 12% increase quarter-on-quarter, accounting for 64% of all ETH held by DATs, equivalent to 3.8% of the total Ethereum supply. In comparison, Strategy holds 4.2% of the total Bitcoin supply.

          Key Performance Indicator: Fair Value

          Summary and Outlook

          The Ethereum network entered a full "reset" mode in the first quarter of this year. Many of the core metrics we track are at historical lows, or have fallen back to early 2023 levels.

          At the same time, network security reached an all-time high. More than $300 billion in assets were protected on-chain. The Foundation refocused on expanding and improving L1 layer security. BitMine continued to acquire assets.

          Meanwhile, regulations targeting traditional finance (TradFi) are becoming clearer, potentially driving the adoption of public blockchains. We believe that for Ethereum to "win" in the next cycle (outperform Bitcoin), it needs to position itself as: the most secure smart contract network (100% uptime) and carrying the most on-chain value (stablecoins, RWA, etc.). Furthermore, Ethereum needs to become the "home of traditional finance," the preferred platform for traditional financial institutions to build on public blockchains. The progress of Robinhood L2 (currently in the testnet phase) is worth noting. Finally, Ethereum also needs to become the public blockchain closest to "quantum computing readiness."

data.title: Reflections and Confusions of a Crypto VC
data.description:
Author: Catrina & Translation: Jia Huan, ChainCatcher

Crypto venture capital is at a watershed moment. In the past three cycles, token exits have been the primary driver of excess returns, but now they are undergoing a major reset. The definition of token value is being rewritten in real time, yet an industry-standard evaluation framework has yet to emerge.

What exactly is happening?

This time, the crypto market structure is being simultaneously impacted and completely upended by multiple unprecedented forces:

1. The emergence of HYPE awakened the token market, proving that token prices can be backed by real revenue, with over 97% of its nine- to ten-figure revenue generated on-chain.

1. The emergence of HYPE awakened the token market, proving that token prices can be supported by real revenue, with over 97% of its nine- to ten-figure revenue generated on-chain.

This completely demystified governance tokens that relied on narratives and lacked fundamentals—think of L1 and "governance tokens" that existed primarily to circumvent the ambiguity of securities laws (which made direct revenue distribution infeasible). HYPE almost overnight reset market expectations: now, revenue is under much stricter scrutiny and has become a basic requirement for entry. 2. The Chain Reaction on Other Token Projects Before 2025, if you had on-chain revenue, you were considered a security; after HYPE, if you ask most hedge funds, they'll tell you that if you don't have on-chain revenue, you're worthless. This has left most projects, especially non-DeFi projects, in a dilemma, forcing them to hastily adapt. 3. PUMP brought a staggering supply shock to the system. The supply explosion caused by the meme frenzy fundamentally disrupted the market structure by diverting attention and liquidity. On Solana alone, the number of newly generated tokens surged from approximately 2,000-4,000 per year to a peak of 40,000-50,000. This effectively eroded the already meager liquidity pie by about one-twentieth. Also seeking excess returns, the same group of buyers shifted their attention and funds to speculating on memes rather than holding altcoins. 4. Retail speculative funds are rapidly diverting. Prediction markets, stock perpetual tokens, and leveraged ETF trading are now directly competing for the same pool of funds that would otherwise flow to altcoins. Meanwhile, the maturity of tokenization technology has made it possible to leverage trading in blue-chip stocks. These stocks, unlike most altcoins, do not face the risk of going to zero and are subject to much stricter regulation, greater transparency, and lower information disadvantage risk. The result is a significant compression of the token lifecycle: the time from peak to trough is drastically shortened, and retail investors' willingness to "hold" tokens has plummeted, replaced by faster capital rotation. Every VC is asking themselves and their peers some big questions: 1. Are we underwriting equity, tokens, or a combination of both? The biggest challenge here is that we don't have a new best practice manual for value accumulation in token projects—even the most successful projects like Aave still face controversy between DAOs and equity. 2. What are the best practices for on-chain value accumulation? The most common example is token buybacks, but that doesn't mean it's right. We have long opposed the prevalent token buyback trend: it's toxic and puts founders with real revenue in a difficult position. This motivation is completely flawed: stock buybacks occur after a company has invested in growth, while cryptocurrency buybacks are increasingly driven by retail/public perception—something completely fickle and irrational—demanding immediate action. You might burn through $10 million that could have been reinvested, only to have that value wiped out the next day by some random market maker being liquidated. Public companies buy back shares when they are undervalued. Token buybacks, on the other hand, are rushed at various stages, often occurring at local highs. Especially if you're a B2B business generating off-chain revenue, this is essentially a waste of time. In my opinion, when your revenue is less than $20 million, there's absolutely no reason to do buybacks just to please retail investors instead of reinvesting the money in growth. I really like this report from fourpillars, which shows that buybacks in the tens of figures do almost nothing to help a project set a long-term price floor. Furthermore, to keep retail investors and hedge funds happy, you have to do buybacks consistently and transparently, like HYPE did. Any failure to do this will be punished, just as PUMP's P/E ratio (based on a fully diluted valuation) is only 6x because the public "distrusts" them—despite the fact that they have actually burned through $1.4 billion in revenue that could have gone into the national treasury. Here is further reading on "On-Chain Value Accumulation Mechanisms That Don't Require Burning Money." 3. Will the "Crypto Premium" Disappear Completely? This means that in the future, all projects will be valued at multiples similar to public stocks (approximately 2 to 30 times revenue). Take a moment to think about what this means—if it comes true, we will see most L1 public chains' prices fall by more than 95% from now, with the exceptions of TRON, HYPE, and other revenue-generating DeFi projects. This is even without considering token attribution. Personally, I don't think that's the case—HYPE set an extremely exceptional expectation, making many investors impatient with the "day one revenue/user traction" of early-stage startups. For sustained innovation like payments and DeFi companies, yes, that's a reasonable expectation. But disruptive innovation takes time to build, launch, and grow before it can achieve exponential revenue growth. In the past two cycles, we've had too much patience and blind optimism for so-called "disruptive technologies"—new L1 public chains, the esoteric concepts of Flashbots/MEV raised funds all the way to rounds 8-9, and now we've overcorrected, only willing to support DeFi projects. The pendulum will swing back. While assessing DeFi projects based on quantitative fundamentals is indeed a net positive for the industry's maturity, qualitative fundamentals also need to be considered for non-DeFi categories: culture, technological innovation, disruptive concepts, security, decentralization, brand equity, and industry connectivity. These qualities are not simply reflected in TVL and on-chain buybacks. What to do now? Expected returns on token projects have been significantly compressed, while equity ventures have not experienced a similar decline. This divergence is particularly evident in early-stage and growth-stage projects. Early-stage investors have become much more price-sensitive when underwriting projects that may exit through tokens. Meanwhile, their appetite for equity ventures has increased, especially in a favorable M&A environment. This is entirely different from the situation in 2022-2024, when token exits were the preferred liquidity path, based on the underlying assumption that token valuation premiums would persist. Later-stage investors—those with the strongest brand equity and added value in the crypto-native context—are increasingly moving away from purely "crypto-native" deals. Instead, they are backing more "Web 2.5" companies, whose underwriting is anchored to revenue traction. This allows them to venture into unfamiliar territory, directly competing with institutions like Ribbit and Founders Fund—who have deeper backgrounds in traditional fintech, stronger portfolio synergies, and better visibility into early-stage deal flows outside of crypto. The crypto VC space is entering a period of value validation. The right to survive depends on VCs finding their own PMF (Product-Market Fit) with the founders, where the "product" is a combination of capital, brand recognition, and added value. For the best deals, VCs need to market themselves to founders to earn the right to be included in the capitalization structure, especially in some of the most successful cases in recent years where projects required little to no institutional capital (e.g., Axiom) or none at all (e.g., HYPE). If capital is the only thing a VC can offer, it will almost certainly be eliminated. Those VCs qualified to remain in the game need to be very clear about what they can offer in terms of brand recognition (the driving force behind the best founders' initial willingness to engage) and added value (which ultimately determines their right to win a deal).

data.title: US-Iran negotiations fall through; Bitcoin battles to defend the $70,000 mark.
data.description:
Author: Ma He, Foresight News

A major rebound in the crypto market remains a long way off. On April 13, the price of BTC hovered around $71,000, down about 2.6% in 24 hours. ETH performed even worse, falling 3.63% to about $2,200. Bitcoin's market capitalization still accounts for 58.8%, and the overall trading volume in the crypto sector has not increased significantly.

Coinglass data shows that in the past 24 hours, the total liquidation amount across the network was $284 million, with long positions liquidated at $203 million. Meanwhile, the CoinMarketCap fear index is currently at 43, indicating a neutral market sentiment.

Traditional financial markets also experienced significant volatility. Affected by the situation in the Middle East, international oil prices remained high, with Brent crude oil once approaching $107 per barrel, a sharp increase from pre-conflict levels. In the US stock market, the S&P 500 fell by approximately 5% in March, one of its worst monthly performances in recent years; international stock markets, dragged down by energy import pressures and a stronger dollar, saw even larger declines. Rising bond yields and renewed inflation expectations led investors to rapidly switch between risky assets, pushing up demand for traditional safe-haven assets such as gold. This cross-market correlation further confirms that cryptocurrencies, as high-beta risk assets, are simultaneously experiencing both macroeconomic and geopolitical shocks.

US-Iran Talks Break Down

One ​​of the key drivers of this week's sluggish cryptocurrency market was the sharp escalation of tensions between the US and Iran. The face-to-face peace talks between the US and Iran, held in Islamabad, Pakistan on April 11-12, lasted over 20 hours but ultimately ended in failure.

US Vice President Vance's delegation and the Iranian delegation failed to reach an agreement on core differences, including Iran's "red line" of abandoning its nuclear weapons program and halting uranium enrichment. Iran, in turn, accused the US of "maximumism" and "constantly changing its objectives."

Following the breakdown of negotiations, US President Trump announced on social media on Sunday that the US military would immediately begin a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. US Central Command (CENTCOM) subsequently confirmed that at 10 PM Beijing time on April 13, the blockade would target all ships entering and leaving Iranian ports, but would not obstruct normal passage through non-Iranian ports. Iran's foreign minister and military quickly responded, warning that any military vessels approaching the strait would be considered a violation of the ceasefire and reserving the right to retaliate. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital chokepoint for global oil transportation, handling approximately 20% of the world's crude oil shipments. If the blockade continues, the risk of supply chain disruptions will directly push up oil prices, further triggering global inflation concerns and expectations of slower economic growth. Such geopolitical black swan events have the most direct impact on risk assets: investors are withdrawing from high-beta assets and turning to cash or gold. BTC's safe-haven properties as "digital gold" are suppressed by risk aversion in the short term, failing to play a hedging role. In past Middle East crises, the crypto market often reacted earlier than traditional markets, experiencing sharp corrections, which is a direct reflection of the current downturn. According to the latest data from Polymarket, the probability of the US and Iran reaching a permanent peace agreement before May 31st this year is 27%, while the probability of reaching an agreement before the 30th of this month has dropped to 14%. However, the future of US-Iran negotiations remains uncertain. According to the Wall Street Journal, informed officials revealed that Middle Eastern countries are vying to bring the United States and Iran back to the negotiating table after marathon peace talks in Islamabad failed to reach an agreement. Despite strong statements from both sides, the diplomatic door remains open, and a second round of talks could take place within days. Regional countries are negotiating with the United States to secure an extension of the fragile two-week ceasefire. Another key factor weighing on the economy comes from monetary policy. The minutes of the Federal Reserve's March meeting showed that despite the high degree of uncertainty brought about by the Iranian conflict, policymakers maintained their expectation of only one interest rate cut in 2026. On April 10th, CPI data was released. The US March unadjusted CPI annual rate was 3.3%, a new high since May 2024, in line with market expectations of 3.3%, compared to the previous value of 2.40%. The US March seasonally adjusted CPI monthly rate recorded 0.9%, a new high since June 2022, in line with market expectations. The US Bureau of Labor Statistics stated that the record rise in gasoline prices contributed nearly three-quarters of the CPI increase that month. Another indicator excluding food and energy costs saw its month-on-month increase slow to 0.2%. The market had initially hoped for a more aggressive easing cycle to boost risk assets, but the oil price shock made the Fed's "data-dependent" strategy more conservative. Higher energy costs could delay the decline in inflation, thus postponing interest rate cuts. This is undoubtedly a further blow to the highly liquidity-dependent crypto market. Historically, the failure of interest rate cut expectations has often been accompanied by a valuation reset of risk assets. BTC's current consolidation around $71,000 is a reflection of this adjustment in macroeconomic expectations. Currently, the latest data from Polymarket shows that the market is betting only a 26% probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates by 25 basis points this year, while the probability of keeping rates unchanged has risen to 44%. Tightening market liquidity casts a shadow over the crypto market's potential rebound. Profit-taking suppresses BTC price rebound. On-chain data metrics reveal the true intentions of crypto players. Glassnode data shows that any attempt to reach the $70,000 to $80,000 range faced insufficient liquidity and profit-taking pressure, thus limiting the rebound's magnitude. Another rebound above $70,000 was exhausted by over $20 million in profit-taking per hour. Perhaps some sellers believe this BTC rebound is merely temporary, not a reversal. Currently, with the BTC price at $70,800, the number of addresses in a loss-making state is approximately 13.5 million. This indicates that a significant portion of users on the network purchased Bitcoin at a price higher than the current spot price. Despite short-term pressure, many crypto industry veterans remain structurally optimistic in the long term. Strategy founder Michael Saylor stated that Bitcoin likely bottomed out around $60,000 in early February, at which point forced traders to sell were shaken out of the market. The bottom was determined more by the exhaustion of sellers than by valuation. He believes current selling pressure is limited, ETF inflows are absorbing daily supply, and continued demand is being generated by companies allocating treasury assets to Bitcoin. Michael Saylor predicts the catalyst for the next bull market will be the formation of a banking and digital credit system built on Bitcoin, which will transform Bitcoin from a non-interest-bearing asset into a capital market engine. BitMEX founder Arthur Hayes stated in late March, "There's a lot of wishful thinking about a positive future in the market right now. Personally, I hope the killing stops, but I won't buy risky assets here." Tom Lee wrote that there are increasing signs that the market has "bottomed out," although the overall market remains skeptical. If you remain skeptical, you might consider buying assets that outperformed during the Iraq War.

data.title: Fake Ledger Wallet App Bypasses Apple Mac App Store Checks and Drains Nearly 6 BTC From US Musician
data.description:
The promise of secure self-custody turned into a costly mistake for one musician, after a convincing fake app slipped through official channels and wiped out years of savings in seconds.

Fake Ledger App Drains Musician’s Bitcoin Holdings

Garrett Dutton, better known as G. Love, has lost nearly 6 Bitcoin after unknowingly installing a malicious app posing as the official Ledger Live software from Apple’s Mac App Store.

The stolen 5.9 BTC was valued at more than $420,000 at the time, representing what he described as a decade of savings intended for retirement.

Dutton explained that he had been setting up his Ledger wallet on a new computer when he searched for the app through the App Store.

The version he downloaded appeared legitimate and guided him through a familiar setup process before requesting his 24-word recovery phrase.

Once entered, the attackers immediately emptied his wallet.

He said,

“I lost 5.9 BTC all I had for ten years I worked on this f#ck be careful out there.”

Source: X

How Did The Scam Slip Through The App Store

The fraudulent app mimicked the official interface closely enough to pass as genuine, raising concerns about how such software made its way onto a trusted platform like Apple’s marketplace.

Ledger has previously warned that even official app stores can host malicious copies designed purely to steal funds, urging users to download its wallet software only through its official website.

This tactic is not new.

Similar fake Ledger apps have surfaced on other platforms in recent years, including Microsoft’s store, where users collectively lost hundreds of thousands of dollars before the listings were removed.

Scammers have expanded their methods beyond app stores as well, using phishing emails, deceptive advertisements, and even physical mail to trick users into revealing sensitive information.

Funds Traced To Exchange But Recovery Unlikely

Blockchain investigator ZachXBT tracked the stolen Bitcoin across multiple transactions, identifying links to deposit addresses associated with KuCoin.

He said the funds were routed through several addresses, possibly via an instant exchange, making recovery difficult.

Hi I traced out your 5.92 BTC stolen and it was all laundered via @kucoincom deposit addresses in the following transactions:

6f5c8eb6b01774626f33527e0cb03c0d1860447acacd6079e69bf41b459bcf1f
9ee1288f941b2c3775ebd125eefeebdc713aa160bf2cf9d18661fd07f84ce891…

— ZachXBT (@zachxbt) April 12, 2026

When asked whether the exchange might intervene, ZachXBT said he did not expect any action.

He also criticised KuCoin’s compliance approach, suggesting it responds selectively depending on circumstances.

The investigator pointed to the exchange’s loss of its MiCA licence in February 2026, only months after receiving it, as a sign of broader regulatory concerns.

He added that illicit actors continue to exploit broker and personal accounts on the platform with limited visible resistance.

Kucoin will not do anything they pretend to be compliant only when it benefits them.

Kucoin lost its MICA license in Feb 2026 after only having it for 3 months for a good reason. https://t.co/S7pMETtSwSpic.twitter.com/NK7SPBxAWI

— ZachXBT (@zachxbt) April 12, 2026

Security Experts Warn Against Sharing Seed Phrases Online

Security professionals have reiterated a long-standing rule in crypto: never enter a hardware wallet seed phrase on any internet-connected device.

Beau, head of security at Pudgy Penguins, warned that attackers rely on urgency and familiarity to trick users into giving up their recovery phrases.

He advised treating any prompt to download or update wallet software with suspicion unless verified independently.

Once a seed phrase is exposed, control of the wallet is effectively handed over, leaving little chance of reversing the loss.

This scam tactic tricks more people than it should

You will NEVER need to enter your hardware wallet seedphrase on an internet connected device (laptop, phone, smart fridge etc)

If you’re restoring a wallet, always do so by entering your seedphrase on a hardware wallet device… https://t.co/IYUV36Pnfa

— Beau (@beausecurity) April 11, 2026

Crypto Fraud Losses Continue To Climb

The incident reflects a wider surge in crypto-related fraud.

Recent FBI data shows that losses in the United States have climbed to $11.36 billion in 2025, alongside more than 181,000 reported complaints.

Crypto Fraud Losses Hit Record High

Americans lost a record $11.36 billion to crypto-related fraud in 2025, a 22% increase YoY.

➡️ Rising adoption is attracting more scams.
➡️ Fraud tactics becoming increasingly sophisticated.
➡️ Regulatory and enforcement pressure likely to… pic.twitter.com/VgHXmAyrYf

— 🌍kwutex Johnson (@De_Okwutex) April 11, 2026

Personal wallet theft alone accounted for hundreds of millions in losses, with many victims losing life savings to increasingly sophisticated scams.

Despite growing awareness, attackers continue to exploit trust in familiar platforms and interfaces, making even experienced users vulnerable—as Dutton himself admitted.

“I been in the crypto circus since 2017. Today they caught me off guard.”

data.title: American Singer G. Love Loses $420K Bitcoin Retirement Fund After Downloading Fake Ledger App From Apple Store
data.description:
American musician Garrett Dutton, better known as G. Love, has shared the story of how he has lost his retirement funds (estimated worth of $420,000) after falling victim to a malicious app scam

Philadelphia Musician G. Love Loses Nearly 6 BTC to Fake Ledger Wallet App on Apple's App Storehttps://t.co/C64ZYaotbN

— Bitcoin.com News (@BitcoinNews) April 12, 2026

Dutton shared how he watched the 5.9 BTC holdings which he had accumulated for nearly a decade disappear right from his eyes as soon as he entered his seed phrase on the malicious application that he downloaded from the Apple app store.

I had a really tough day today I lost my retirement fund in a hack/Scam when I switched my @Ledger over to my new computer and by accident downloaded a malicious ledger app from the @Apple store. All my BTC gone in an instant.

— G. Love (@glove) April 11, 2026

While Dutton regarded himself a veteran after spending almost 10 years in what he describes as the "crypto circus", he admitted the attack did catch him off guard.

"It was my own damn fault for not being more diligent. But let it serve as a warning. There's so many scams."

Blockchain Trail Leads To KuCoin

In a follow-up post, crypto sleuth investigator ZachXBT later revealed that the stolen funds were rerouted across nine transactions to deposit addresses associated with KuCoin.

Fake Ledger app on Apple’s Mac App Store just wiped out a musician’s entire 5.92 $BTC retirement in minutes . . . 🫣

The victim was American musician G Love.@zachxbt said the stolen BTC was routed through KuCoin deposit addresses.

🔒 Critical Safety Rules:

▸Never download… pic.twitter.com/ZioWq2o0Q7

— BeInCrypto (@beincrypto) April 13, 2026

While the exchange responded publicly, there has been no indication that the funds were recovered.

The case adds to a growing trend of crypto-related crime. According to the FBI, U.S. losses tied to crypto scams surpassed $11 billion in 2025, reflecting a sharp rise in fraudulent activity across the sector.

The FBI just confirmed that 2025 was the most expensive year in history for crypto scams. Nobody is talking about how the average loss per person skyrocketed because AI deepfakes are now perfectly mimicking trusted voices. Digital assets are being drained by sophisticated… https://t.co/2ECJ9YlamF

— KingZephyr (@KingZahyd) April 7, 2026

The scam relied on a malicious clone of Ledger Live, designed to trick users into entering their seed phrase—the master key that grants full access to a crypto wallet. Once exposed, funds can be transferred instantly and irreversibly.

Although the fake app was no longer visible on Apple’s App Store at the time of reporting, similar incidents have occurred before. In 2023, attackers used a fake Ledger app on Microsoft’s store to steal nearly $600,000 in Bitcoin, after bypassing platform review systems.

A Stark Reminder For Crypto Users

Dutton’s experience underscores a critical reality in crypto: self-custody offers full control—but also full responsibility.

Even with hardware wallets and established security practices, a single mistake—such as entering a seed phrase into an unverified app—can result in total loss.

$BTC is still trading around the $70,500 level.

It is increasingly unlikely that we will retest the $73K level, with price action suggesting further downside from here.

A break of $70,500 will result in significantly more downside, so this is an important level to watch! https://t.co/p8cVabXp6jpic.twitter.com/hJL7PnXZy9

— Wealthmanager (@Wealthmanager) April 13, 2026

His message to followers was simple but urgent: stay vigilant.

As crypto adoption grows, scams are becoming more sophisticated—targeting not just newcomers, but also long-time holders with significant assets.

Dutton’s loss is more than an isolated incident. It reflects a broader challenge for the industry: making self-custody both secure and user-friendly, without exposing users to devastating risks.
